name: SLTF Spanish Archer
classification_notes: An uppercase-focused display serif built entirely around an extensive ligature system of over 200 connections, making it a decorative display face rather than a functional text typeface.
summary: An uppercase-only display serif with over 200 ligatures that lock and overlap characters to simulate custom lettering automatically.
description: Built around a single structural idea, the face uses its ligature table to connect and overlap characters so that typeset words take on the appearance of hand-lettered work without manual intervention. A single Regular weight is offered, with OpenType features covering lining and oldstyle figures, proportional and tabular variants, a stylistic set, and the extensive standard ligature library. Best suited to logotypes, headlines and packaging where the interlocking character connections can be read at scale.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe Spanish Archer as a ligature-rich display serif in which letters refuse to stand alone, with over 200 ligatures connecting, overlapping and locking characters so that every typeset word becomes a small piece of custom lettering with no manual adjustment required.

name: SLTF The Brachen
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes it as a calligraphic editorial serif; the italic is drawn as a genuine separate cut with calligraphic strokes and fluid connections rather than a slanted roman, placing it firmly in the calligraphic serif tradition.
summary: A two-cut calligraphic editorial serif with a separately drawn italic that shifts character considerably from the roman.
description: The family comes in just two styles, Regular and a true italic, the latter drawn from scratch with calligraphic strokes and fluid letter connections rather than simply slanting the roman. OpenType features are generous for a small family, covering small capitals, oldstyle and tabular figures, several stylistic sets, and an extensive ligature set that includes combinations such as Th, ct and ff sequences. Aimed at editorial, fashion and luxury branding contexts where the calligraphic flavour of the italic can be used expressively against the more upright roman.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe The Brachen as a calligraphic editorial serif offered in a Regular and a genuine True Italic, stressing that the italic is not a slanted roman but a separately drawn cut with calligraphic strokes, fluid connections and letterforms that shift personality entirely. It is positioned for editorial and premium design work, supported by professional-grade OpenType features throughout.

name: SLTF Dessign Maison
classification_notes: Self-described as a hybrid display typeface that merges rounded sans letterforms with calligraphic serif elements in a single design; the blended nature makes display the most fitting primary category.
summary: A single-weight display typeface that blends rounded sans letterforms with calligraphic serif details, aimed at premium branding and packaging work.
description: Offered as one file in a single Regular weight, it combines rounded sans construction with calligraphic serif strokes in a design SilverStag position as an alternative to conventional display type. Two stylistic sets and a range of ligatures give some typographic flexibility within the constraints of a single style. The Latin Extended glyph set, oldstyle figures and scientific inferiors suggest an ambition beyond purely decorative use.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Dessign Maison as a hybrid display typeface where rounded sans letterforms and calligraphic forms coexist in a single file, designed for designers who want something with more personality than a conventional display choice.

name: SLTF Arcilla
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a contemporary editorial serif, Arcilla spans nine weights with matching italics and variable font delivery, placing it squarely in the modern workhorse serif category aimed at editorial and branding contexts.
summary: A contemporary editorial serif spanning nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, and available as variable fonts for flexible deployment.
description: Built for editorial, branding and packaging work, Arcilla covers a wide weight range with consistent italic companions at every step. Two variable fonts extend the family further, allowing smooth interpolation across the full weight axis. OpenType features include oldstyle and tabular figures, multiple stylistic sets, fractions and standard ligatures, giving it practical depth for long-form and display use alike.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe Arcilla as a variable contemporary editorial serif built for the full range of editorial, branding and packaging work, offering nine weights from Thin to Black each with a matching italic, plus two variable fonts. It began as a single weight before expanding into the complete family available today.

name: SLTF Curo
classification_notes: A single-weight super-heavy display sans built around geometric forms, offered in five corner style variants that move from sharp to rounded rather than weight to weight.
summary: A single extreme-weight display sans offered in five corner style variants, from sharp-edged to fully rounded, within one family purchase.
description: Rather than varying weight, Curo varies corner treatment across five named styles: Sharp, Crisp, Regular, Soft and Rounded. This gives a tonal range suited to brand work where mass and presence are fixed requirements but character needs to shift. Coverage extends across Latin Extended with OpenType features including oldstyle figures, fractions and alternates.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Curo as a super heavy display sans designed for brands that demand presence, built around a single extreme weight with five distinct corner style variants giving designers a complete tonal range within one family.

name: SLTF The Silver Editorial
classification_notes: A high-contrast serif with expressive weight range across 9 weights plus italics, drawing on the bold display serifs of 1980s fashion magazine typography rather than classical text tradition.
summary: An 18-font editorial serif drawn from 1980s fashion magazine typography, available in nine weights with matching italics.
description: Inspired by the expressive, high-contrast serifs found in 1980s fashion publishing, the family runs from Thin to Black with corresponding italic cuts, totalling 18 fonts. OpenType features include oldstyle figures, lining figures, case-sensitive forms and an extensive ligature set covering ct, et, ff, fi, fl and several others. The weight range makes it usable across display headlines, packaging and branding work without switching families.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type position The Silver Editorial as a high-impact serif typeface inspired by expressive 1980s fashion magazine typography, fully redrawn and expanded into an 18-font system of 9 weights plus italics, intended for editorial design, branding, packaging and digital storytelling.

name: SLTF Netsera
classification_notes: Netsera is a calligraphic serif with inktraps, flourishes, and swash alternates drawn from calligraphic tradition, placing it firmly in the calligraphic serif subcategory rather than a conventional transitional or humanist serif.
summary: A calligraphic serif in two styles, Regular and Italic, carrying over 900 glyphs including 170+ alternates and ligatures aimed at luxury and editorial applications.
description: Built around calligraphic letter construction with inktrapped uppercase variants and extensive OpenType alternates, Netsera offers small caps, oldstyle figures, flourished lowercase letters and calligraphic alternate forms for both upper and lowercase. The glyph set covers Latin Extended with broad diacritic support, making it usable across Western and Central European languages. It is aimed at high-end packaging, magazine editorial and romantic visual identities where decorative flexibility is expected.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe Netsera as a calligraphic editorial serif designed to bring elegance to every letterform, packed with over 900 characters including 170+ alternates and ligatures. They position it for luxury branding, premium packaging, magazine spreads and romantic visual storytelling, with both Regular and Italic versions full of character, detail and what they call gentle sophistication.

name: SLTF Rettiko
classification_notes: A bold condensed display typeface drawing from classic condensed display typography traditions with a retro character; designed purely for headline impact rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight bold condensed display face drawing from classic retro condensed typography for headline and poster use.
description: Built around a narrow, high-contrast structure with nostalgic flavour, it targets posters, packaging and campaign headlines where tight condensed setting is needed. OpenType features include oldstyle figures, lining figures, case-sensitive forms and a stylistic set offering alternate letterforms for C, G, O and Q. Latin Extended character coverage makes it usable across a broad range of Western European languages.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Rettiko as loud, unapologetic and built to command attention, citing classic condensed display typography as its source and framing it as a contemporary reinterpretation that balances nostalgic energy with modern precision. They position it for headlines, posters, branding, packaging and digital campaigns.

name: SLTF The Elfkin Editorial
classification_notes: High contrast between thick and thin strokes and elegant proportions place this firmly in the modern serif tradition, oriented towards editorial and display use rather than extended text.
summary: A high-contrast modern serif from SilverStag Type with an extensive ligature set aimed at editorial and branding work.
description: Available in Regular and Italic, the family ships with over 80 ligatures and alternates, oldstyle figures, stylistic sets and case-sensitive forms via OpenType. The high stroke contrast and refined proportions suit fashion, beauty and luxury branding contexts where a polished serif is required. Coverage extends across Latin Extended, including Vietnamese, with broad diacritic support.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe The Elfkin Editorial as a modern serif designed for impactful editorial work, stylish branding and expressive typographic storytelling, offering elegant proportions, high contrast and a beautifully crafted italic alongside more than 80 ligatures and alternates.

name: Silver Garden
classification_notes: The page describes a vintage-inspired serif with both upright and italic cuts, extended ligatures, and OpenType alternates, placing it in the transitional serif tradition with display-leaning nostalgic qualities.
summary: A four-style serif family from SilverStag Type built around a vintage aesthetic, with extensive ligatures and OpenType alternates aimed at branding and packaging work.
description: Offered in Regular, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic, the family leans on a nostalgic serif character with an extended ligature set covering combinations such as th, ff, fi and fl, plus a full suite of stylistic alternates. Coverage spans basic Latin and Latin Extended-A, making it suited to Western European languages. Positioned at the softer end of retro-revival type, it fits label, identity and editorial projects where period warmth is the primary requirement.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Silver Garden as a unique and heartfelt nostalgic typeface balancing vintage charm with modern sophistication, inspired by timeless elegance and designed with a personal touch. They offer it as a versatile font duo suited to a wide range of creative applications.

name: SLTF Knolt
classification_notes: A single-weight ultra-condensed display face with high contrast and ink traps, designed explicitly for headline and poster use rather than text setting.
summary: An ultra-condensed single-weight display face with sharp ink traps, high contrast and an extensive ligature set aimed at fashion and editorial headlines.
description: Built entirely around a narrow, high-contrast silhouette, it features ink traps and precision-cut curves suited to large-scale headline work. The OpenType feature set is unusually broad for a display face, with hundreds of standard ligatures covering two-letter and three-letter combinations alongside oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms and a stylistic set offering alternate I, J and N. Latin Extended character support makes it usable across a range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe SLTF Knolt as a bold ultra-condensed display font built for maximum impact, citing its sharp ink traps, dramatic contrast and precision-cut curves as giving it a high-fashion, high-energy character. They position it as a font designed to command attention through a sleek, intentional silhouette.

name: Officially Funky
classification_notes: A font duo pairing clean sans-serif letterforms with alternate serif letters, designed for expressive display use with a nostalgic, playful character rather than text setting.
summary: A single-weight font duo from SilverStag Type pairing clean sans-serif forms with alternate serif letters, aimed at playful branding and packaging work.
description: The duo blends two contrasting styles — a clean sans-serif and a serif variant with alternate letterforms — unified by a nostalgic, upbeat personality. OpenType features include discretionary ligatures, standard ligatures and a full set of stylistic alternates accessible via all-alternates. Coverage extends across Latin and Latin-1 Supplement, with standard punctuation and currency symbols included.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Officially Funky as a bold and playful modern font duo for designers who want to blend nostalgic charm with contemporary flair. Following their earlier releases French Lovers and Silver Garden, the duo fuses clean sans-serif letterforms with alternate serif letters to give projects a fresh, characterful feel.

name: SLTF Night Windfall
classification_notes: The family is presented as a sans and serif duo with matching metrics; the sans component drives the classification as a geometric-leaning sans-serif, with the serif variant sold as a companion rather than the primary face.
summary: A matched sans and serif duo with shared metrics, sold as a pairing solution for branding and packaging work.
description: The family ships as two fonts — a sans and a serif — drawn to identical metrics so they can be layered, swapped or contrasted without manual adjustment. The glyph set covers Latin Extended with a wide range of OpenType ligatures built in. Aimed at branding, editorial and packaging designers who want a ready-made pairing rather than sourcing two typefaces independently.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe SLTF Night Windfall as a sophisticated font duo built for designers who value balance, versatility and high-end aesthetics. The release pairs an original sans with a newly crafted serif companion drawn to identical metrics, enabling seamless pairing, layering and contrast across a single project.

name: SLTF The Roman Edition
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a classical serif inspired by ancient Roman letterforms, with classical proportions and delicate detailing placing it in the old-style or classical serif tradition.
summary: A two-style classical serif with Roman-inspired proportions, aimed at luxury branding and editorial packaging work.
description: Available in Regular and Italic, it draws on ancient Roman letterform traditions and pairs classical proportions with OpenType features including oldstyle figures, swash alternates, an alternate lowercase s and a set of standard ligatures covering ct, st and the ff series. The glyph set covers Latin Extended with broad diacritic support for Central and Eastern European languages. Positioned squarely at luxury packaging, high-end branding and editorial layout.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe The Roman Edition as a sophisticated serif inspired by the grandeur of ancient Roman letterforms, merging classical proportions with delicate detailing for luxury branding, editorial layouts, high-end packaging and refined digital design.

name: Amelie Fierce
classification_notes: A high-contrast serif with sharp strokes and pixel-inspired details positioned explicitly as a display face; the strong weight contrast and decorative ligature system place it firmly in the display category rather than text serif.
summary: A single-weight high-contrast display serif with over 280 ligatures and pixel-inflected details aimed at fashion and editorial branding.
description: Amelie Fierce pairs sharp, high-contrast serifs with small pixel-inspired accents across a glyph set that includes oldstyle figures, case-sensitive forms and an unusually large library of standard ligatures covering hundreds of letter-pair combinations. The design targets high-fashion branding, packaging and editorial headings where strong typographic personality is required at large sizes. Latin Extended coverage supports a broad range of Western European languages.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag describe Amelie Fierce as a bold, elegant display serif built to challenge convention and spark creativity, combining high-fashion sophistication with digital rebellion through sharp stroke contrast, graceful curves and over 280 ligatures.

name: Lightshift
classification_notes: A bold, script-inflected display face with thick strokes and retro curves; its expressive, personality-driven forms place it firmly in the display category with a retro funky subcategory rather than any neutral text classification.
summary: A bold retro display typeface in four styles, mixing thick strokes and script-inspired curves with outline and rounded variants.
description: Four styles span Regular, Rounded, Regular Outline and Rounded Outline, each sharing the same thick-stroke, curve-heavy construction that references 1970s display lettering. Two stylistic sets and a range of standard ligatures give designers alternate letterforms, particularly for A through Z and script-like joins. Suited to packaging, poster and branding work where a high-energy retro register is wanted.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe Lightshift as bold and expressive, radiating energy and personality through a playful blend of retro curves, thick strokes and script-inspired details. They position it as a fun, funky and modern choice for designers wanting to make an impact, available across four distinct styles in the complete family.

name: Raving
classification_notes: Extreme stroke contrast between hairline and thick strokes, intended exclusively for display use in fashion, editorial and packaging contexts, placing it firmly in the high-contrast display serif category.
summary: A single-weight high-contrast display serif with extreme stroke drama, 110+ ligatures and stylistic alternates, aimed at fashion and editorial work.
description: Extreme contrast between razor-thin hairlines and heavy strokes gives this display serif its defining character, making it well suited to fashion mastheads, packaging and editorial headlines. A single Regular weight ships with over 110 ligatures, stylistic alternates, lining and oldstyle figures, and case-sensitive forms, giving designers substantial typographic flexibility within a single cut. Coverage spans Latin Extended-A and a selection of Latin Extended-B characters, with a small set of stylistic alternates for K, R, h, m and n.
foundry_blurb: SilverStag Type describe Raving as a bold editorial serif crafted for high-impact typography, with extreme contrast between hairline and thick strokes making it suitable for fashion, branding, editorial layouts and standout packaging. They highlight the 110+ ligatures and stylistic alternates as tools for distinctive, statement-making typography.
